# YOLOmatic

> Automated computer-vision training CLI/TUI for YOLO26, YOLOv12, YOLO11,
> YOLOv10, YOLOv9, YOLOv8, YOLOX, RF-DETR, SAM 3.1, and Detectron2.

Install:

```sh
uv tool install yolomatic
yolomatic
```

Repository development:

```sh
git clone https://github.com/shahabahreini/YOLOMatic.git
cd YOLOMatic
uv sync
uv run yolomatic
```

## What it does

YOLOmatic helps practitioners and researchers configure, train, fine-tune,
predict, benchmark, augment, convert, monitor, and upload computer-vision models
without writing repetitive training boilerplate.

Concrete examples:

- Generate a YOLO26, YOLO11, YOLOv8, YOLOX, RF-DETR, SAM 3.1, or Detectron2
  training config from an interactive terminal wizard.
- Fine-tune `.pt`, `.pth`, or HuggingFace checkpoints on a custom dataset.
- Convert Labelbox or Ultralytics-platform NDJSON exports to YOLO or COCO.
- Benchmark checkpoints with mAP, F1, per-image rankings, and UMAP vectors.
- Run SAM 3.1 auto, text-prompted, or box-prompted segmentation.
- Upload YOLO checkpoints or deploy RF-DETR checkpoints to Roboflow.

## Documentation index

- README: https://github.com/shahabahreini/YOLOMatic#readme — concise project landing page.
- Docs home: https://shahabahreini.github.io/YOLOMatic/ — rendered documentation site.
- Install: https://shahabahreini.github.io/YOLOMatic/getting-started/install/ — install with uv and configure optional credentials.
- Quickstart: https://shahabahreini.github.io/YOLOMatic/getting-started/quickstart/ — launch the TUI and run common commands.
- First training run: https://shahabahreini.github.io/YOLOMatic/getting-started/first-training-run/ — generate a config and train.
- Datasets: https://shahabahreini.github.io/YOLOMatic/guides/datasets/ — YOLO, COCO, Labelbox, and Ultralytics-platform data.
- Models: https://shahabahreini.github.io/YOLOMatic/guides/models/ — supported model families and benchmark tables.
- YOLO guide: https://shahabahreini.github.io/YOLOMatic/guides/yolo/ — model selection, export, and deployment guidance.
- RF-DETR: https://shahabahreini.github.io/YOLOMatic/guides/rf-detr/ — RF-DETR training and fine-tuning.
- SAM 3.1: https://shahabahreini.github.io/YOLOMatic/guides/sam/ — SAM inference and fine-tuning.
- Benchmarking: https://shahabahreini.github.io/YOLOMatic/guides/benchmarking/ — HTML reports, mAP, F1, and UMAP.
- Cloud upload: https://shahabahreini.github.io/YOLOMatic/guides/cloud-upload/ — Roboflow upload and deployment.
- CLI commands: https://shahabahreini.github.io/YOLOMatic/reference/cli-commands/ — command entrypoint reference.
- Configuration: https://shahabahreini.github.io/YOLOMatic/reference/configuration/ — generated YAML config reference.
- Smart split: https://shahabahreini.github.io/YOLOMatic/reference/smart-split/ — rare-class-aware dataset splitting.
- NDJSON conversion: https://shahabahreini.github.io/YOLOMatic/reference/ndjson-conversion/ — Labelbox and Ultralytics NDJSON conversion.
- FAQ: https://shahabahreini.github.io/YOLOMatic/faq/ — common questions.
- Comparison: https://shahabahreini.github.io/YOLOMatic/comparison/ — YOLOmatic vs Ultralytics CLI, Roboflow, and hosted tools.
- Citation: https://shahabahreini.github.io/YOLOMatic/citation/ — CFF and BibTeX citation metadata.
- Changelog: https://shahabahreini.github.io/YOLOMatic/changelog/ — release history.
- Contributing: https://shahabahreini.github.io/YOLOMatic/contributing/ — development setup and PR conventions.

## Supported model families

- YOLO26: detection, segmentation, classification, pose, and OBB variants where
  supported by Ultralytics.
- YOLOv12: attention-centric YOLO family, useful for research and benchmarking.
- YOLO11: stable production YOLO family.
- YOLOv10: NMS-free detection family.
- YOLOv9: detection and segmentation variants.
- YOLOv8: detection, segmentation, classification, pose, and OBB variants.
- YOLOX: detection variants.
- RF-DETR: native detection and segmentation training with `.pth` checkpoints, plus a preview keypoint/pose model (RFDETRKeypointPreview) trained from COCO keypoint JSON.
- SAM 3.1: open-vocabulary segmentation and mask decoder fine-tuning.
- Detectron2: Faster R-CNN, RetinaNet, and Mask R-CNN configs.

## Common questions

### What is YOLOmatic?

YOLOmatic is a Python 3.12 command-line toolkit with an interactive terminal UI
for computer-vision training workflows.

### Is YOLOmatic only for YOLO?

No. It supports multiple YOLO generations plus RF-DETR, SAM 3.1, and Detectron2.

### Does YOLOmatic require CUDA?

No. CUDA is recommended for training, but CPU and Apple Silicon MPS fallbacks are
supported. The TUI detects common CUDA/PyTorch mismatches.

### Can YOLOmatic convert Labelbox NDJSON?

Yes. It converts Labelbox and Ultralytics-platform NDJSON exports to YOLO or
COCO.

### Can YOLOmatic fine-tune RF-DETR?

Yes. It discovers `.pth` checkpoints and routes RF-DETR configs to the native
RF-DETR trainer.

### Can YOLOmatic upload to Roboflow?

Yes. YOLO checkpoints can be uploaded and RF-DETR checkpoints can be deployed
through the Roboflow upload workflow.

```bibtex
@software{bahreini_jangjoo_yolomatic_2026,
  author = {Bahreini Jangjoo, Shahab},
  title = {{YOLOmatic}: automated computer-vision training CLI for YOLO, RF-DETR, SAM, and Detectron2},
  year = {2026},
  version = {4.4.0},
  license = {Apache-2.0},
  url = {https://github.com/shahabahreini/YOLOMatic},
  note = {Python CLI/TUI for configuring, training, fine-tuning, predicting, benchmarking, and uploading computer-vision models}
}
```
